summary: Visibility is the deciding advantage, provided the convention also controls drift and reading-surface clutter.

points.text: A distant database is queryable but invisible beside the artifact, has separate access and history, and can rot without appearing in ordinary review.
Overloaded frontmatter or comments couple metadata to file-specific parsers and can break renderers or consumers.
A stale sidecar can misdescribe current content; fingerprints, `lastReviewedAt`, explicit status and age-based review make drift observable.
Visible companions become visual noise when document-reading surfaces do not filter machinery by default.
